seoTitle: How to Disable Protected View in Microsoft Excel
metaDescription: Turn off the Enable Editing banner in Excel by adjusting Trust Center settings, unchecking specific Protected View filters, or trusting local folders.
intro: Opening a workbook every day only to click through the same "Enable Editing" banner gets old fast. This guide covers navigating to Excel's Trust Center to adjust or fully disable Protected View filters, plus setting up trusted local folders as a more targeted alternative.

steps.heading: Open the Options menu
steps.body: Click the File tab, then scroll to the bottom of the sidebar and click Options.

steps.heading: Go to the Trust Center
steps.body: In the Options window, click into the Trust Center tab in the sidebar.

steps.heading: Open Trust Center Settings
steps.body: Click the Trust Center Settings button to open the detailed security configuration.

steps.heading: Find Protected View
steps.body: Click the Protected View row in the settings menu.

steps.heading: Uncheck the filters you want to disable
steps.body: Uncheck the checkboxes for files from the internet, unsafe locations, or Outlook attachments as needed.

steps.heading: Or trust specific folders
steps.body: Alternatively, use the Trusted Locations tab to whitelist specific local folders instead of disabling Protected View entirely.

faq.question: Is it safe to fully disable Protected View?
faq.answer: Disabling it removes a security layer that isolates potentially unsafe files, so it's generally safer to whitelist trusted folders instead of turning it off completely.

faq.question: Why do I need to restart Excel after changing this setting?
faq.answer: Trust Center changes typically require a relaunch of the application to fully apply across newly opened files.

faq.question: Can I disable Protected View for just email attachments?
faq.answer: Yes, the three checkboxes let you selectively target internet downloads, unsafe locations, or Outlook attachments individually.

faq.question: What's the difference between disabling filters and using Trusted Locations?
faq.answer: Disabling filters turns off the warning broadly, while Trusted Locations lets you whitelist specific folders and keep protection active everywhere else.
